data.title: Sebi readies settlement scheme for brokers in algo violation. Zerodha, 5Paisa, Motilal Oswal among 110 brokers served with show-cause notice
data.description:
Sebi readies settlement scheme for brokers in algo violation. Zerodha, 5Paisa, Motilal Oswal among 110 brokers served with show-cause notice

India’s market regulator, the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI), is set to roll out a settlement scheme for stock brokers who received show-cause notices over their association with unregulated algo platforms, according to sources familiar with the matter.

The scheme, which has been under consideration since the issuance of notices, will be presented to SEBI’s board on March 24 as an information memorandum and is expected to be notified soon after, one of the sources said. “The scheme is ready and will be placed before the board in the upcoming board meeting as an information memorandum and will be notified there after,” the source said. The regulator aims to resolve the matter swiftly rather than letting it escalate into a prolonged legal issue akin to the NSEL or illiquid options cases, another source added.

With the violations being considered more of an industry-wide practice than a major infraction, SEBI is keeping the settlement amount low, in the range of ₹1-2 lakh. Brokers seeking to avail of the scheme must apply to SEBI, and the window will likely remain open for three months, with the possibility of an extension based on response levels.

Last year, SEBI issued show-cause notices to more than 110 stock brokers, including major players like Zerodha, 5Paisa Capital, and Motilal Oswal Financial Services, after observing that unregulated platforms were offering algo-based trading strategies marketed with claims of assured returns. Platforms like Tradetron facilitated these trades by linking their algorithms to broker systems, despite SEBI’s explicit directives against such associations.

To safeguard investors, SEBI issued a statement in June 2022 and followed up with a circular in September, warning brokers against associating with unregulated platforms offering algorithmic trading strategies. The circular, titled “Performance/Return Claimed by Unregulated Platforms Offering Algorithmic Trading Strategies,” explicitly barred brokers from referencing past or expected returns of such strategies or collaborating with platforms making such claims. It also mandated brokers to sever ties with these platforms within seven days.

Despite the directive, SEBI found continued broker affiliations with these platforms, leading to an investigation and subsequent show-cause notices to over 110 brokers. Alongside the new settlement scheme, SEBI is also working on a standardized process for handling such cases to ensure transparency and a uniform enforcement approach.

Amid rising retail interest in algo trading and the proliferation of unregulated platforms, SEBI introduced fresh regulations on February 4, placing the onus on brokers as principals for algo trading executed via application programming interfaces (APIs). Under the new rules, algo providers and fintech vendors will act as agents of brokers and must be empanelled with stock exchanges. Brokers will be prohibited from onboarding non-empanelled algo providers.

SEBI did not immediately respond to an email from Moneycontrol seeking comment.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Zee Entertainment shares rise 6% as CLSA sees stock price doubling in 12-24 months
data.description:
Zee Entertainment shares rise 6% as CLSA sees stock price doubling in 12-24 months

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of Zee Entertainment Enterprises Ltd (ZEEL) on March 20 jumped up to 6% after brokerage firm CLSA said it sees the stock price doubling in next 12-24 months.

The foreign brokerage believes advertising revenue-led growth may trigger rerating on the counter, as it suggested a target price of Rs 170.

ZEE's EBITDA margin has widened by 9 per cent from lows and even after assuming 6 per cent year-on-year (YoY) ad growth, ZEE may deliver 22-23 per cent growth in EBITDA and profit after tax in FY26-27 compounded annually, CNBC-TV18 reported CLSA as saying.

On March 20, Zee Entertainment shares on BSE rose 6% to trade at Rs 106.84 apiece before paring gains to trade at Rs 102.77 apiece.

The brokerage expects the stock to double over the next 12-24 months as its market cap-to-sales ratio of 1x is at a 60% to 80% discount to the Reliance-Disney JV and Sun TV.

Zee is India's No 2 TV network and is ramping up OTT via Zee5 platform, it said.

CLSA said ZEEL stock is at "rock bottom" 8x PE.

In January 2024, Sony had pulled out from the proposed $10.5-billion merger with ZEE Entertainment Enterprises Ltd citing failure to meet certain “closing conditions” by the Indian firm, which led to massive fall in the ZEEL stock.

ZEEL and Sony had agreed to merge on December 22, 2021.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: 'Indian investors allocate more to gold than their global counterparts and this strategy has paid off'
data.description:
ETFs have grown rapidly, surpassing mutual funds in assets and marking a record year. In 2024, ETFs accounted for $1.5 trillion of the $2 trillion flowing into managed investments globally.

When it comes to investing, globally, investors are moving towards exchange traded funds (ETFs), especially in the active ETF space, according to Wing Chan, Head of Manager Research, Asia-Pacific, Morningstar Asia.

“Indian investors have historically allocated more to gold than their global counterparts, and this strategy has paid off, given gold’s strong performance in recent years — even in a strong US dollar environment. Globally, however, investors tend to have lower exposure to gold,” Chan told Moneycontrol on the side lines of Morningstar Awards for Investing Excellence India event.

Edited excerpts:

How are India and other Asian countries placed when it comes to foreign investors for ETFs?
I think a lot of that, from what we can see, are more domestically focused or domestically orientated. Because if you are a US investor or European investors, you are able to find those ETFs in their local markets to invest in. And they generally are fairly broad based. So, eventually the money will flow to Asia because there are ETF vehicles that buy into Asia or emerging markets. But those tend to be listed in their local time zone, so it is easy for them to trade. I think what we're seeing more within sort of Asian listed ETFs primarily, at least on the retail side, more domestic investors.

We are talking about Gold and Silver ETF investing in India. What trends are you seeing beyond India?
Indian investors have historically allocated more to gold than their global counterparts, and this strategy has paid off, given gold’s strong performance in recent years—even in a strong US dollar environment. Globally, however, investors tend to have lower exposure to gold. One key reason is that gold doesn’t generate cash flow like equities, fixed income, or real estate. Its price movements are purely driven by supply and demand dynamics, making it harder to assess intrinsic value. In contrast, global investors favour securitised assets where future cash flows can be forecasted, such as listed equities, bonds, or even REITs.

Globally, what kind of investing trends are you seeing?

ETFs have grown rapidly, surpassing mutual funds in assets and marking a record year. In 2024, ETFs accounted for $1.5 trillion of the $2 trillion flowing into managed investments globally. Key drivers include market performance, low-cost investing, and regulatory support. Strong global markets (in countries like US and India) have fuelled this momentum. While ETFs were once primarily passive investments like S&P 500 trackers, active ETFs are now gaining ground, particularly in the US, where portfolio managers actively pick stocks. This shift has further boosted ETF inflows.

The US remains the dominant ETF market, even attracting Asian investors for its liquidity and depth. Meanwhile, Asia’s ETF market is expanding, with India seeing significant domestic flows and other key regional markets following suit.

What are the ETF trends in other Asian markets?

Apart from India, Japan and China are two major ETF markets experiencing rapid growth. After decades of deflation, Japan is now seeing inflationary pressure, prompting investors to protect their purchasing power. A tax-advantaged investment scheme has further accelerated ETF inflows, especially into low-cost options, following Central Bank actions.

China’s ETF market has also surged in recent years. Slowing economic growth and the underperformance of active managers have driven investors toward cost-effective ETFs. Regulatory support has further strengthened this shift, making ETFs an increasingly attractive option.

Are there any particular types of ETFs that are seeing a growth in demand?

In Japan, the weaker Yen has driven investors toward ex-Japan ETFs, with global and US equity being the most popular. The market is also benefiting from tax-advantaged investment schemes, further boosting ETF inflows. Low-cost ETFs continue to gain traction, supported by central bank actions in recent years.

In China, strict capital controls keep ETF investments domestic, focusing on large-cap blue chip stocks with strong dividends. Mid-cap companies, which once provided opportunities for active managers, are slowing, pushing investors further toward passive ETFs. Regulatory support has also strengthened this shift.

Taiwan’s ETF market is driven by dividend-paying, income-focused products, appealing to investors looking for stable returns. There is also growing interest in thematic ETFs, particularly in technology and semiconductor sectors, given Taiwan’s dominance in this space.

In Australia, passive ETFs dominate due to cost efficiency and a mature market with fewer opportunities for active managers to add value. However, interest in active ETFs is rising, with some fund managers launching new products to tap into this growing demand.

What about thematics?

Thematic funds saw a major boom from 2020 to 2022, particularly in the European Undertakings for Collective Investment in Transferable Securities (UCITS) market, which enjoys wide distribution. In Asia, strong inflows targeted technology, electric vehicles, and ESG themes, with India, Japan, China, Hong Kong, and Singapore showing significant interest. This demand extended beyond global markets into domestic ones, further fuelling growth.

However, recent trends indicate outflows, especially in cross-border markets. Thematic investing requires not just picking the right fund but also the right theme, which is challenging. As some themes lose momentum, investors are becoming more selective, leading to a shift in flows.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Macrotech Developers Shares Surge 2.39% on High Trading Volume
data.description:
During today's session, Macrotech Developers experienced a notable surge, climbing 2.39% to reach Rs 1206.25. This increase was accompanied by significantly higher trading volumes, suggesting strong investor interest and market activity. The stock's movement has drawn attention as it impacts several key indices.

The surge in Macrotech Developers is particularly noteworthy due to its inclusion in multiple indices, including the NIFTY NEXT 50, NIFTY 500, NIFTY 100, NIFTY REALTY, Nifty 200, Nifty LargeMidcap 250, and Nifty Total Market. Such broad index representation means that the stock's performance can have a cascading effect on various investment portfolios and market benchmarks.

Financial Performance Snapshot


A review of Macrotech Developers' recent financial performance reveals the following:
  • Revenue: The company's revenue has shown variability over the past few quarters, with the most recent figure for December 2024 standing at Rs 3,485.30 Cr. Other quarterly revenues include Rs 2,560.50 Cr (Sep 2024), Rs 2,785.40 Cr (Jun 2024), Rs 3,526.00 Cr (Mar 2024), and Rs 2,793.90 Cr (Dec 2023).
  • Net Profit:Macrotech Developers' net profit has also fluctuated. The net profit for December 2024 was Rs 564.70 Cr, compared to Rs 385.10 Cr (Sep 2024), Rs 465.70 Cr (Jun 2024), Rs 539.20 Cr (Mar 2024), and Rs 313.20 Cr (Dec 2023).
  • Earnings Per Share (EPS): The EPS figures reflect these changes, with the latest EPS for December 2024 at Rs 5.67, versus Rs 3.87 (Sep 2024), Rs 4.68 (Jun 2024), Rs 5.54 (Mar 2024), and Rs 3.25 (Dec 2023).

Annual Financial Highlights


Looking at the annual data, Macrotech Developers has demonstrated the following:
  • Revenue: Annual revenue has increased steadily over the past five years, from Rs 8,114.76 Cr in 2020 to Rs 9,459.50 Cr in 2024.
  • Net Profit: The company's net profit has seen considerable variation, with a low of Rs 122.14 Cr in 2021 and a high of Rs 1,163.80 Cr in 2024.
  • EPS: The annual EPS has also fluctuated, ranging from Rs 3.09 in 2021 to Rs 24.77 in 2022, and settling at Rs 12.04 in 2024.

Balance Sheet Summary


Key balance sheet figures as of March 2024 include:
  • Share Capital: Rs 994 Cr
  • Reserves & Surplus: Rs 15,999 Cr
  • Total Liabilities: Rs 47,624 Cr
  • Total Assets: Rs 47,624 Cr

Key Financial Ratios


Important financial ratios for Macrotech Developers include:
  • Basic EPS (Mar 2024): Rs 12.04
  • Book Value per Share (Mar 2024): Rs 170.88
  • Debt to Equity (Mar 2024): 0.51

Sentiment Analysis


Recent sentiment analysis indicates a neutral stance. The sentiment on March 19, 2025, was neutral with a closing price of Rs 1177.45, and remained neutral on March 20, 2025, with a closing price of Rs 1205.45.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers shares surge 7% on MoU with Nagaland government; up 35% in a week
data.description:
GRSE shares have rallied a staggering 35 percent in just a week.

Shares of Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers (GRSE) extended their rally on Thursday, March 20, rising over 7 percent to Rs 1,760 after the company signed a fresh Memorandum of Understanding (MoU). This follows a 20 percent surge in the stock on Wednesday.

The state-owned defence shipbuilder announced a deal with PWD Nagaland for the supply of eight double-lane modular Steel bridges to improve infrastructure in the state. This adds to Garden Reach’s growing portfolio, having previously delivered modular bridges to the Border Roads Organisation (BRO), the National Highway Infrastructure Development Corporation Ltd. (NHIDCL), several state governments, and neighbouring countries like Bhutan, Nepal, Myanmar, Sri Lanka, and Bangladesh. The company has supplied over 5,800 modular bridges to date.

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

Beyond infrastructure projects, Garden Reach is making strides in naval shipbuilding. The company recently completed Contractor Sea Trials for two warships—Himgiri and Androth—both set to join the Indian Navy.

It delivered a solid performance in the December quarter, posting robust revenue and profit growth alongside margin expansion. The company’s revenue jumped 37 percent year-on-year to Rs 1,271 crore, while net profit rose 12 percent to Rs 98 crore. EBITDA surged 55 percent to Rs 75.63 crore, with margins improving by nearly 70 basis points to 5.95 percent from 5.27 percent in the same period last year.

Also read: Polycab, Havells, KEI shares plunge up to 5% on Adani's foray into cables and wires

The momentum in defence and shipbuilding stocks has been broad-based. Peers like Mazagon Dock and Cochin Shipyard also surged on Wednesday, buoyed by comments from Fincantieri CEO Pierroberto Folgiero, who highlighted India’s rising prominence as a global shipbuilding hub. Fincantieri is among the world's largest shipbuilders, and its endorsement has further fueled investor optimism toward the sector.

At about 11 am, shares of the company were trading at Rs 1,7254, higher by 5 percent from the last close on the NSE. GRSE shares have rallied a staggering 35 percent in just a week.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before making any investment decisions.

data.title: Paras Defence shares rise 10% on winning DRDO order worth Rs 142 crore
data.description:
Paras Defence shares rise 10% on winning DRDO order worth Rs 142 crore

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Paras Defence share price jumped 10 percent intraday on Thursday after winning an order from the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRD) of over Rs 140 crore.

Shares of Paras Defence and Space Technologies advanced 9.84 percent to an intraday high of Rs 1,047.7 per share on the NSE. The scrip opened gap-up with a gain of 2.22 percent in today's trading session. The counter has been rising for the last three trading sessions and is up nearly 14 percent.

The company in an exchange filing, said "Paras Defence and Space Technologies Limited has received an order from Center for High Energy Systems & Sciences (CHESS), DRDO, Ministry of Defence, Government of India, Hyderabad valued at approximately Rs 142.31 crores for Development of Laser Source Module and Integration with Beam Control System (BCS) on Mobile Platform."

"This is a part of the order of High-Power Laser System meant for Anti Drone and Anti Missile applications," it added.

data.title: Paytm shares crash 6% as Jefferies maintains 'hold', sees marginal upside
data.description:
Paytm shares have crashed nearly 30 percent since the start of the year.

Shares of One 97 Communications Paytm plunged as much as 6 percent to Rs 718 in morning deals on March 20 after international brokerage Jefferies maintained its 'hold' call on the counter suggesting a few roadblocks that could hinder the growth prospects of the company.

With a price target of Rs 850 per share, analysts forecast an upside potential of 11.4 percent from the last close of Rs 763 per equity share. Paytm shares have crashed nearly 30 percent since the start of the year.

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

For FY25, government incentives for low-value UPI P2M transactions have been halved to Rs 1,500 crore, reducing incentives from 20 bps to 6 bps. If Paytm’s incentives decline proportionately, its adjusted EBITDA for FY25 could be 50 percent below estimates, while FY26-27 projections maybe 20-30 percent lower, Jefferies said.

Pre-tax profit (PBT) for FY25 could also see a 15 percent hit. Jefferies will closely monitor the company’s shift to MDR-based charges for large merchants, which could boost profitability and enhance revenue predictability.

Also read: Bulls roar on D-Street: Sensex jumps 500 pts, Nifty reclaims 23,000 post-Fed outcome; IT stocks surge

The development gains significance as this comes just a few days after it received SEBI's approval on Paytm Money providing services as a research analyst. Therefore, now the payment services platform owned by One-97 Communications will be able to provide SEBI-compliant research services, including investment insights, research reports, and data-driven analysis.

Read more: IT, FMCG, auto, financials see most outflows from FIIs in first half of March

Earlier this month, Paytm had also launched automatic payment deductions from bank accounts for trading on equity broking apps. ‘UPI Trading Blocks’ is a part of an automated process that is based on NPCI’s UPI infrastructure wherein funds are deducted during trade execution. It doesn't require the users to enter a UPI PIN for transactions at each time of its occurrence. This setup provides greater transparency, as the money stays in the investor’s bank account until required for a trade.

At about 10:20 am, shares of the company were trading at Rs 728, lower by 5 percent from the last close on the NSE.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before making any investment decisions.

data.title: Sensex soars over 550 pts, Nifty reclaims 23,000 intraday as Fed eases tariff concerns, other factors drive market rally
data.description:
Sensex soars over 550 pts, Nifty reclaims 23,000 as Fed eases tariff concerns, other factors drive market rally

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Equity benchmarks surged for the fourth consecutive session on Thursday, tracking firm global cues after the US Federal Reserve reaffirmed its rate cut projections for this year. A rally in information technology (IT) stocks and steady domestic buying momentum lifted market sentiment.

Sensex climbed 564.77 points or 0.74 percent to an intraday high of 76,013.82, while the broader NSE Nifty advanced 163.5 points or 0.71 percent to 23,071.10. The rally was led by IT heavyweights as the Nifty IT index gained 2.2 percent in early trade.

Among the top gainers on the Nifty, three of the top five stocks were IT majors—Wipro, Infosys, and Tata Consultancy Services—which rose nearly 3 percent. Bharti Airtel and Eicher Motors were among the other gainers.

Key Factors Driving Market Gains

1) Fed's Dovish Tone on Tariffs and Inflation: The Federal Reserve indicated that it remains open to cutting interest rates later this year despite concerns over inflationary pressures from tariffs. Fed Chair Jerome Powell stated that the impact of tariffs on inflation is likely to be temporary, calming fears among investors. Powell also downplayed recession risks, saying they were “not high,” which helped ease market nerves.

“Markets will read this as a slightly dovish stance, with the Fed not overly concerned about economic slowdown or inflation. Both stocks and bonds reacted positively,” Bloomberg quoted Christian Hoffmann of Thornburg Investment Management as saying.

2) Domestic Consumption and Beaten-Down Sectors Gaining Momentum: Market experts highlighted that domestic consumption-driven stocks continued to find favour. Several stocks, including Kotak Mahindra Bank, Bajaj Finance, InterGlobe Aviation, and Muthoot Finance, hit 52-week highs in recent sessions, reflecting strong investor interest in these segments.

“Beaten-down themes like defence and shipping are also witnessing a revival, while consumer-facing digital stocks remain on a strong footing,” said V K Vijayakumar, Chief Investment Strategist at Geojit Financial Services.

Stock Market LIVE Updates

3) Strong US Market Cues: Overnight, US stock indices ended with solid gains as the Fed’s policy stance reassured investors. The Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 383.32 points or 0.92 per cent to 41,964.63, the S&P 500 rose 60.63 points or 1.08 per cent to 5,675.29, and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite advanced 246.67 points or 1.41 per cent to 17,750.79.

The positive sentiment extended to US futures, with Nasdaq and S&P 500 futures edging up 0.4 per cent and 0.3 per cent, respectively. However, major Asian markets showed mixed trends, with Japan’s Nikkei 225 and China’s CSI 300 trading lower by 0.40 per cent and 0.46 per cent, respectively, while Hong Kong’s Hang Seng Index slipped 1.5 per cent.

Technical Outlook:

Anand James, Chief Market Strategist at Geojit Financial Services, projected volatility within the 23,100-22,730 range in the near term. He suggested that if the index slips below 22,800, it could signal a larger corrective move. Meanwhile, resistance is seen in the 23,460-23,500 zone, with 23,807 identified as the next short-term target.

Market participants will closely monitor global cues and institutional flows for further direction in the coming sessions.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: IREDA launches first-ever perpetual bonds to raise Rs 1,247 crore
data.description:
IREDA

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

State-owned renewable energy developer and financier Indian Renewable Energy Development Agency (IREDA) has launched its first-ever perpetual bond, to raise Rs 1,247 crore, triggering a modest uptick in early trade on March 20, however the shares have cooled off and slipped into the red. Additionally, the company has also received a tax refund of Rs 24.48 crore.

In an exchange filing shared on March 20, IREDA said the perpetual bonds have been issued at an annual coupon rate of 8.4 percent. "This landmark initiative marks a major step in optimizing the company's capital structure while capitalizing on the current supportive market conditions," IREDA said.

The PSU called the issuance of perpetual bonds a 'strategic move' to enhance Tier-I capital to back the expansion of green energy infrastructure. Speaking on issuance, IREDA Chairman and Managing Director Pradip Kumar Das said, "Strengthening our capital base through Perpetual Bonds will enable us to scale up financing for renewable energy projects, accelerating India’s transition to a cleaner and more sustainable future."

This development comes days after IREDA's board decided to hike the borrowing limit for FY25 to Rs 29,200 crore from Rs 24,200 crore. The increased in borrowing will be through instruments like taxable bonds, perpetual debt instruments, term loan from banks, lines of credit from international agencies, external commercial borrowing, short terms loans from banks and more.

IREDA on March 20 also announced that it has received a tax refund of Rs 24.48 crore on March 19 from the Income Tax Department. The refund is related to the partial relief granted by the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) for Assessment Year (AY) 2011-12. The company further added that it is yet to receive nearly Rs 195 crore worth of refund, as approved by the named authority for AYs 2010-11, 2012-13, 2013-14, and 2015-16 to 2018-19.

After falling over 55 percent from its July peak, IREDA shares have seen a strong comeback in the recent days, snapping its six-day losing streak on March 19.
data.author: MoneyControl
